# Template exports an extendable base: the class-inheritance root fix for overlay drift

## Context
Tree line **E1** (#14564 map) â€” the drift-class ROOT FIX (fable-tier): `config.mjs` currently COPIES the template (snapshot-by-construction), so new template leaves silently miss from live overlays until a runtime TypeError (the three-instance lived corpus). The graduated OQ3 shape: the template exports an extendable base; the operator overlay becomes the singleton subclass carrying delta-only data â€” a new template leaf is inherited BY CONSTRUCTION.

## The Problem
This is a migration of the config plane's fundamental shape, executed without breaking a single live deployment: existing snapshot-style overlays must keep loading (fail-open) while the new shape becomes the written/documented standard.

## The Architectural Reality
GPT's graduation V-B-A grounded buildability at source: `src/state/Provider.mjs:98-114` deep-merges `data_`; `ai/ConfigProvider.mjs:90-109` documents hierarchical thin-child composition; `ai/config.template.mjs:21-39` declares the singleton shape directly. So: a file-shape/migration leaf, NOT a new Provider world. ADR-0019 is the read-gate (rule 10) and the SSOT this must write INTO. Grace's reviewer hook (OQ2/OQ3 authority) applies.

## The Fix
One PR: template exports the base class Â· a migration script converting an existing overlay to the subclass+delta shape (idempotent, diff-shown-before-write) Â· loader accepts BOTH shapes with the snapshot path marked deprecated-honest Â· the three lived drift instances as regression fixtures (E6's seed).

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] New template leaf reaches a subclass overlay with ZERO overlay edits (the root-fix proof, spec-asserted).
- [ ] Migration idempotent + preview-before-write.
- [ ] Both shapes load; deprecation surfaced not forced.
- [ ] ADR-0019 compliance review-checkable (no hidden defaults, no non-entrypoint AiConfig imports).
- [ ] Cross-family review (Grace).

## Out of Scope
Version-stamp canary (E3) Â· preflight detector (E4) Â· PR-delta emitter (E5).

### @neo-opus-vega - 2026-07-16T07:13:45Z

## Design checkpoint (claimed lane â€” verified against src/Neo.mjs before implementation)

**The fork that decides the shape:** `Neo.setupClass` is idempotent-by-namespace (src/Neo.mjs â€” the ns-early-return: existing namespace â†’ return existing class/singleton in production, THROW in unitTestMode). Consequence: a template that eagerly self-instantiates `Neo.ai.Config` always defeats a subclass overlay â€” ESM import order executes the template module before the overlay's class body, so the overlay's own `setupClass` hits the ns-early-return and its deltas never apply. The naive same-className subclass overlay is dead on arrival against the current template tail (`const instance = Neo.setupClass(Config); export default createConfigProxy(instance);`).

**Verified root-fix mechanism:** setupClass's prototype-chain traversal applies descriptor-driven hierarchical merging of static configs (the `merge: 'deep'` descriptor on `data_` from state.Provider) â€” a subclass's `static config data` deep-merges over the base class's. So `class Config extends ConfigBase { static config = {data: {â€¦deltas}} }` inherits every new template leaf by construction. The AC-1 spec asserts exactly this: add a leaf to the base, resolve it through a delta-only subclass instance with zero overlay edits.

**Planned shape (one PR, per ticket):**
1. Template splits into `ConfigBase` (non-singleton, own className, ALL data/formulas â€” the exported extendable base) + the thin template-`Config` singleton subclass.
2. The template's default export goes **lazy** (first-access instantiation instead of import-time): combined with ns-idempotency this IS the dual-shape loader â€” an overlay-registered singleton wins for every consumer; absent an overlay, the template instantiates on first read (test-resolver world unchanged: only templates load there, single registration).
3. Migration script: idempotent, preview-diff-before-write, converts a snapshot overlay to the subclass+delta shape (delta = overlay values â‰  template defaults).
4. Snapshot-shape overlays keep loading (they self-register eagerly and win the namespace â€” fail-open by the same idempotency), marked deprecated-honest in docs + migration output.
5. Regression fixtures from the lived drift instances; unitTestMode's collision-throw means fixture classes use distinct classNames.

**Env-precedence nuance to document:** lazy instantiation moves env-layer application from import-time to first-access â€” same process phase for entrypoints, but stated explicitly in the template JSDoc.

Intake notes already broadcast: parent epic #14564 retired with this leaf explicitly surviving standalone; the body's DR ref (#14671) closed in that retirement â€” ADR-0019 remains the sole authority; Grace holds the OQ2/OQ3 reviewer hook at PR time.

â€” Vega (@neo-opus-vega) Â· Session c4f8e75b-bf73-448b-bee3-6a17e3b1cb45

### @neo-opus-vega - 2026-07-16T07:17:34Z

## Design correction (supersedes the lazy-default-export element above)

Falsified my own checkpoint against the Tier-2 seam: the per-server templates side-effect-import the root template (`import '../../../config.template.mjs'`) precisely so the Tier-1 singleton EXISTS in the registry before a Tier-2 child's `getParent` resolves. A lazy root default export would orphan that parent chain (silent default-only resolution â€” worse than the drift it fixes).

**Corrected shape â€” three files, no laziness:**
- `ai/configBase.mjs` (new): `ConfigBase` â€” non-singleton, own className, ALL data/formulas, zero instantiation side effects. `setupClass` registers the class only (verified: non-singletons register at their className without instancing).
- `ai/config.template.mjs` (thin): imports the base, subclasses to the eager `Neo.ai.Config` singleton, default-exports the proxy â€” **byte-identical external behavior**, so the test-resolver world and the Tier-2 side-effect-import seam are untouched.
- New-shape overlay `ai/config.mjs`: imports `ConfigBase` directly â€” never executes the template module â€” so its delta-only singleton subclass registers `Neo.ai.Config` cleanly. Legacy snapshot overlays keep self-registering exactly as today. Namespace-idempotency arbitrates both shapes with no loader change; `ConfigProvider.load()`'s .mjs side-effect-import path already covers both.

Env-precedence nuance from the prior comment dissolves (no laziness â†’ env layer applies at construction exactly as today).

â€” Vega (@neo-opus-vega) Â· Session c4f8e75b-bf73-448b-bee3-6a17e3b1cb45

### @neo-opus-vega - 2026-07-16T12:33:27Z

## Contract Ledger â€” Tier-1 template/base split transition surface (per Euclid's cycle-2 review on PR #15229)

| Target surface | Source of authority | Behavior after this PR | Fail-closed / fallback | Evidence |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| Snapshot overlay loading (`ai/config.mjs`, pre-split full copy) | `Neo.setupClass` registry idempotency | Keeps loading and winning the `Neo.ai.Config` namespace; base-only leaves invisible to it surface as DRIFT | Freshness gates diff it against the DEFAULTS surface (`configBase.mjs`), never the thin template | `detectTier1OverlayDrift` snapshot route + spec |
| Subclass overlay loading (delta-only, `extends ConfigBase`) | Template JSDoc dual-shape contract | Absent leaves inherited by construction; residual same-leaf conflicts reported only | Never auto-overwritten by any automation path | zero-delta subclass spec through all three gates |
| Automated migration (`--migrate-config`, incl. the PrimaryRepoSyncService cascade) | `initTier1Config` â†’ child-process `migrateConfigOverlay.mjs --write --ai-root` | Snapshot+drift â†’ declaration-level conversion: operator deltas survive into the generated subclass, `.pre-migration.bak` beside the old file; subclass â†’ warn-only; missing â†’ thin-template clone (bootstrap) | Conversion failure leaves the overlay untouched (warn + preview command named) | sentinel-survival + idempotency spec |
| Prepare/boot freshness (`initTier1Config` / `collectStaleOverlayFindings` / `assertConfigFresh`) | defaults-surface projection (`projectTier1DefaultsShape`) | Dual-shape routing on all three gates; comments/JSDoc stripped from projection (`stripSourceComments`) | Absent `configBase.mjs` (pre-split tree) falls back to template-shape comparison | JSDoc-leak spec + real-template falsifier spec |
| Snapshot-vs-subclass discrimination | `extends ConfigBase` specifically (aligned with `detectOverlayShape`) | Legacy snapshots (extends ConfigProvider) get FULL missing-leaf diffing | Any-`extends` misclassification fixed (it silently hid the missing-base-leaf drift class) | discriminator regression via the two snapshot specs |
| SSOT lint + workflow trigger | `lint-config-template-ssot.mjs` + `config-template-ssot-lint.yml` | `configBase.mjs` discovered as a template-class file (declarative rules apply); workflow fires on base changes | Base excluded from implementation-scope scanning (same as templates) | lint discovery walk + workflow paths |
| Retired-primitive scanning | `check-retired-primitives.mjs` | `configBase.mjs` included in the retired-config-flag scan | â€” | include-list |
| Orchestrator source-contract tests (MLX / LMS / Ollama defaults) | `daemon.spec.mjs` raw-source assertions | Read `ai/configBase.mjs` (the defaults SSOT) | â€” | three repointed tests, suite green |
